Market Review continued on next page 3 | April 2025 Market Review Equity Market Update April 2025 saw broad based rally for Indian equity markets. While NIFTY 50 / BSE Sensex Index ended the month with 3.5% and 3.7% gain respectively, mid cap index witnessed a sharper rise. Oil and Gas, FMCG, Banks and Autos were the key outperforming sectors while capital goods, IT and Metals underperformed. US administration’s pause on reciprocal tariffs and subsequent FII buying influenced market sentiments in April. Major global equity markets witnessed mixed trend during the month. While equity markets in Korea, Germany and Japan ended the month with gains, markets in US, UK, France and China declined. Further, Market cap-to-GDP stood ~93% (based on CY25 GDP estimates) term risks include global trade uncertainties and resultant moderation in global and the gap between 10Y G-sec yield and 1Y-Forward NIFTY 50 Index earnings yield* has reduced recently [*Earnings yield = 1/ (one year forward P/E)]. growth, cyclical moder ation in corporate earnings, slowdown in government’s reforms momentum etc. Debt Market Update 10 year Gsec yield fell ~22bps in April to 6.36% and touched intra month low of 6.32%. Key events / news which influenced yields include second rate cut by RBI and stance change from neutral to accommodative and OMO purchase of G-secs. The 10-year corporate bond spreads over Gsec widened by 2bps compared to last month. The corporate bond yields spreads over Gsec have come down recently as liquidity conditions have improved significantly due to RBI’s measures. In line with our positive outlook on Gsec yield, we expect the corporate bond yields to fall and spreads to compress further over the medium term. Falling inflation and further potential policy rate cuts could be positive from yields perspective. Thus, in view of convergence of short-term rates, attractive corporate bonds spreads (over Gsec), expectations of more rate cuts and improved liquidity, one may consider investment in medium duration (schemes with duration of upto 5 years) categories especially corporate bonds focussed funds in line with individual risk appetite. Further, investors with a relatively longer investment horizon, could continue with their allocation to longer duration funds in line with individual risk appetite.
